didTheyPass.name: Did They Pass?
didTheyPass.description: Write a function that analyzes driving test marks and determines if the student passed. A student fails if they have any major faults (💥) or 5 or more minor faults (❌).

dangerous.name: Danger to society
dangerous.description: We can't let this one on the road!

oneBigMistake.name: One big mistake
oneBigMistake.description: One big mistake is all it takes to fail!

scrapedThrough.name: Scraped through
scrapedThrough.description: They cut it close but they passed!

oneMistakeTooMany.name: One mistake too many!
oneMistakeTooMany.description: All those little errors added up.

lookAtEachMark.question: How do I look at each individual mark?
lookAtEachMark.answer: Loop through the marks string one character at a time. Each character is either ✅, ❌, or 💥.

emojiInQuotes.question: Why is my code complaining about 💥?
emojiInQuotes.answer: Emoji like 💥 and ❌ still need to be wrapped in quotation marks. They're characters in a string just like letters are.

majorFault.question: What should I do when I see a 💥 (major)?
majorFault.answer: Any single major fails the test, no matter what else has happened. So you can return `false` straight away as soon as you see one. There's no need to keep looking.

minorCount.question: How do I handle minors, where the count matters?
minorCount.answer: Keep a counter variable and add 1 to it each time you see a ❌. Ignore the ✅s, they don't change anything.

finalCheck.question: What do I check at the end?
finalCheck.answer: Once the loop's finished and you haven't seen a major, the test passes if there were fewer than 5 minors. Return `true` if the minor count is less than 5, otherwise `false`.
